title: Who We Are - BGD e-GOV CIRT
description: Bangladesh e-Government Computer Incident Response Team (BGD e-GOV CIRT), the designated National CERT operating under the Bangladesh Computer Council (BCC). No explicit post-quantum / quantum-safe cryptography content found on site.

title: BGD e-GOV CIRT Policies
description: Policy/advisory listing page of the national CERT. No advisory or guidance explicitly naming post-quantum cryptography, PQC, or quantum-resistant migration was found.

title: Meeting on Bangladesh Cybersecurity Strategy 2021-2025 Responsibility Matrix - BGD e-GOV CIRT
description: Bangladesh Cyber Security Strategy 2021-2025. National strategy aligned to Digital Bangladesh pillars; no explicit mention of post-quantum / quantum-safe cryptography in available descriptions.

title: Bangladesh Government adopts cyber security declaration - BGD e-GOV CIRT
description: Government cyber security declaration notice. No explicit post-quantum cryptography reference.

title: ICT Division Reform Roadmap / National Digital Transformation Strategy 2025-2030 (draft)
description: ICT Division (Ministry of Posts, Telecommunications and IT) national ICT roadmap. Lists 'quantum technologies' as a priority research area but does not explicitly address post-quantum / quantum-safe cryptography or PQC migration.

title: Made in Bangladesh - ICT Industry Strategy 2022
description: ICT Industry Strategy 2022 listing quantum technologies among priority research areas. Generic 'quantum' mention only; no explicit PQC / quantum-safe cryptography reference. Mirror host, not an official .gov.bd domain.

title: Quantum-Proofing the Future: Cipher Core Taps Bangladesh (newsletter)
description: Vendor newsletter from Cipher Core (Japanese company) referencing a Bangladesh engagement on quantum-proofing. Explicit PQC framing but issuer is a foreign commercial vendor, not a Bangladeshi institution.

title: Quantum computing revolution: Is Bangladesh preparing to seize the future? - Prothom Alo
description: Op-ed in Bangladeshi newspaper Prothom Alo on quantum computing readiness. Journalism/opinion, not an institutional source; discusses quantum computing generally.
